config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena justru hak individu adalah batas agar mayoritas tidak semena-mena. Dalam pengalaman saya, kebijakan yang baik itu mencari keseimbangan: melindungi kepentingan umum tanpa mengorbankan kebebasan, martabat, dan perlindungan hukum setiap orang. Kalau hak individu terlalu mudah dikalahkan atas nama masyarakat, itu membuka jalan bagi penyalahgunaan kekuasaan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my American perspective, individual rights are the guardrails that keep majority rule from becoming mob rule or government overreach, and history here shows how badly things can go when people are told their liberties matter less for the "greater good." That said, rights are not absolute in every situation, because public safety, epidemics, and basic law enforcement sometimes require limits. But the default should be that society has to justify any restriction on individual rights, not the other way around.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara otomatis di atas hak individu. Dalam konteks Indonesia, kita memang hidup dengan nilai gotong royong, tetapi gotong royong bukan berarti individu boleh dikorbankan seenaknya oleh mayoritas atau oleh negara. Justru hak individu itu penting sebagai pagar agar alasan “demi masyarakat” tidak dipakai untuk membungkam kritik, membatasi kebebasan, atau membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Yang paling masuk akal menurut saya adalah menyeimbangkan keduanya, dengan pembatasan hak hanya jika benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not outrank individual rights by default, because in the United States we have enough history to know how easily “the common good” can become an excuse for abuse. I do not reject social responsibility, and I do think rights can be limited for real public safety needs like disease control or criminal law, but those limits should be narrow, evidence-based, and temporary. The burden should be on society and the government to prove why a right must be restricted, not on the individual to prove why their liberty deserves respect. If we flip that standard, we weaken the very protections that keep majorities from steamrolling minorities and dissenters.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak tegas gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus mengalahkan hak individu secara default. Dari pengalaman saya melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal gotong royong seharusnya tidak berarti hak orang bisa diinjak oleh mayoritas atau negara. Hak individu perlu jadi batas yang jelas, sementara pembatasan hanya sah kalau benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan bisa diuji. Jadi, yang tepat bukan mendahulukan masyarakat secara mutlak, melainkan menyeimbangkan keduanya dengan perlindungan kuat terhadap kebebasan dan martabat orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over individual rights as a default rule. From my American perspective, that kind of principle is dangerous because it gives governments and majorities too much room to claim they are acting for the common good while trampling dissent, minority rights, and personal liberty. I do think society can justify limits in real emergencies like pandemics or serious public safety threats, but those limits have to be narrow, evidence-based, and temporary. The burden should always be on society to prove why a right must be restricted, because once you reverse that standard, abuse becomes much easier.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
